MentionV2.description:
A single sanitized mention returned by V2 data endpoints.

V2 never returns raw tweet text or content — use `link` to fetch the original
tweet if needed. `mentionedAt` is an ISO 8601 string.

tweetId.description: Tweet ID (string to preserve precision across 64-bit IDs).

mentionedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (e.g. "2026-04-18T15:07:49+00:00"). Not a unix timestamp.

repostBreakdown.description: Reposts broken down by audience: `smart` = smart accounts, `ct` = crypto-Twitter accounts.

KeywordMentionV2.description:
Mention returned by `/v2/data/keyword-mentions`. Includes minimal account
info; no follower counts, display name, bio, or account ID are returned.

tweetId.description: Tweet ID (string to preserve precision across 64-bit IDs).

mentionedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (e.g. "2026-04-18T15:07:49+00:00"). Not a unix timestamp.

repostBreakdown.description: Reposts broken down by audience: `smart` = smart accounts, `ct` = crypto-Twitter accounts.

account.description:
Minimal account info for the author. Only `username` and `isVerified` are
returned — no id, display name, follower count, or bio. May be omitted if
the underlying record is missing an account name.

metadata.description:
Pagination metadata. `cursor` is a millisecond timestamp — pass it back as
the `cursor` query param to fetch the next page. Absent on the final page.
`total` may be capped at 10000 for performance.

symbol.description: Token ticker symbol (e.g. "BTC", "$ETH"). Required for tokenIntro / tokenAnalysis when contractAddress is not provided.

chain.description: Blockchain name (e.g. "ethereum", "solana"). Use together with contractAddress as an alternative to symbol.

contractAddress.description: Token contract address. Use together with chain as an alternative to symbol.

message.description: The user message. Only used when analysisType is "chat"; ignored for all other analysis types.

sessionId.description: Omit to start a new conversation. Pass the sessionId from a previous response to continue an existing conversation.

analysisType.description:
The type of analysis to perform. Defaults to "chat".
- `chat` — conversational AI. Requires `message`.
- `macro` — macro market overview.
- `summary` — quick market summary.
- `tokenIntro` — token introduction. Requires `assetMetadata`.
- `tokenAnalysis` — in-depth token analysis. Requires `assetMetadata`.
- `accountAnalysis` — Twitter/X account analysis. Requires `assetMetadata.username`.

assetMetadata.description: Required for tokenIntro, tokenAnalysis, and accountAnalysis. See endpoint description for required fields per analysis type.

sessionId.description: Session ID for continuing this conversation

supported.description: Whether the symbol is tradable as a Hyperliquid perpetual

Record_string.unknown_.description: Construct a type with a set of properties K of type T

title.description:
Optional title to validate alongside the query. Accepted so agents can
validate the full create-query payload in one call. See
{@link ApiKeyCreateQueryRequestBody.title}.

description.description:
Optional description to validate alongside the query. See
{@link ApiKeyCreateQueryRequestBody.description}.
description.example: Notify when BTC is above 100k so I can review the breakout setup.

title.description:
Short human-readable title for the query.

Surfaced back in outbound notifications (Telegram, webhook, notify) so the
recipient immediately recognizes **what** fired — recipients often see an
alert hours or days after the query was created. Strongly recommended for
agent-built queries.

description.description:
1–2 sentence description of the thesis or intent behind the trigger.

Surfaced alongside the title in outbound notifications so the recipient
understands **why** the trigger was set up. Write it as if the recipient
has forgotten they set this up — because they often have.
description.example: Notify when BTC is above 100k so I can review the breakout setup.

evaluatedAt.description: ISO timestamp of the latest evaluation

wouldTriggerNow.description: Whether the latest evaluation would trigger at that time

error.description: Optional execution error object from Athena (when present)

executions.description: List of executions triggered by this query

credits.description: Aggregated LLM credits from sessions linked to this query

title.description:
Short human-readable title for the draft.

Carried over when the draft is converted into an active query and then
surfaced in outbound notifications (Telegram, webhook, notify). Set this
at draft time so downstream recipients know **what** the alert is about.

description.description:
1–2 sentence description of the thesis or intent behind the draft.

Surfaced alongside the title in outbound notifications once the draft is
activated. Write it as if the recipient has forgotten they set this up.
description.example: Notify when BTC is above 100k so I can review the breakout setup.

sessionId.description: Session ID for continuing this conversation

message.description: AI response in Markdown. EQL will be in a JSON code block when generated.

credits.description: Approximate credits equivalent (rounded to whole credits)

stepId.description: Deterministic step id (Athena requires exactly one action step)

type.description: Action type to execute when conditions are met

title.description:
Short human-readable title for the query.

Surfaced back in outbound notifications (Telegram, webhook, notify) so the
recipient immediately recognizes **what** fired — recipients often see an
alert hours or days after the query was created. Strongly recommended for
agent-built queries.

description.description:
1–2 sentence description of the thesis or intent behind the trigger.

Surfaced alongside the title in outbound notifications so the recipient
understands **why** the trigger was set up. Write it as if the recipient
has forgotten they set this up — because they often have.
description.example: Notify when BTC is above 100k so I can review the breakout setup.

evaluatedAt.description: ISO timestamp of the latest evaluation

wouldTriggerNow.description: Whether the latest evaluation would trigger at that time

error.description: Optional execution error object from Athena (when present)

executions.description: List of executions triggered by this query

get.description: Simple health check to verify API availability
Retrieve the current status and usage of your API key

This experimental endpoint is under active development and its behavior may change without notice.
Use with caution in production environments.

Retrieve trending tokens based on highest mentions count,
filtered by a minimum mention threshold over a specified time period.

Note: You must provide EITHER timeWindow OR both from and to parameters.
If both timeWindow and from/to are provided, from/to takes priority.

This experimental endpoint is under active development and its behavior may change without notice.
Use with caution in production environments.

Retrieve smart stats given username

parameters.description: Account username to get stats for
parameters.name: username

get.description:
Returns the most significant mentions for a given ticker symbol, ranked by
engagement (viewCount → mentionedAt → relevance) within a specified time
window.

**Response shape.** The response envelope is `{ success, data, metadata }`.
`data` is a flat array of mentions; pagination state lives in `metadata`
(`total`, `page`, `pageSize`) — never merged into the top level.

**No tweet content.** V2 does not return raw tweet text. Use the `link`
field to fetch the original tweet via the Twitter/X API if needed, or use
the SDK's `fetchRawTweets: true` option with a Twitter bearer token.

**No account object.** Unlike `/v2/data/keyword-mentions`, this endpoint
does not return an `account` object per mention.

**Field notes.** `mentionedAt` is an ISO 8601 string (not a unix
timestamp). `repostBreakdown.smart` counts reposts by smart accounts;
`repostBreakdown.ct` counts reposts by crypto-Twitter accounts.

parameters.description: The ticker symbol to get mentions for. Prefixing with `$` will only return cashtag matches.
parameters.name: ticker

get.description:
Search mentions by keywords OR account name within a time period. Either
`keywords` or `accountName` (or both) must be provided.

**Response shape.** The response envelope is `{ success, data, metadata }`.
`data` is a flat array of mentions; pagination state lives in `metadata`.

**Cursor pagination.** Unlike `/v2/data/top-mentions` (which uses
`page`/`pageSize`), this endpoint is cursor-paginated.
`metadata.cursor` is a millisecond timestamp (number). To fetch the next
page, pass its value back as the `cursor` query param.
`metadata.cursor` is absent on the final page. `metadata.total` may be
capped at 10000 for performance.

**No tweet content.** V2 does not return raw tweet text. Use the `link`
field to fetch the original tweet via the Twitter/X API if needed, or use
the SDK's `fetchRawTweets: true` option with a Twitter bearer token.

**Account info.** Each mention includes a minimal `account` object with
only `username` and `isVerified`. No id, display name, follower count, or
bio is returned.

**Field notes.** `mentionedAt` is an ISO 8601 string (not a unix
timestamp). `repostBreakdown.smart` counts reposts by smart accounts;
`repostBreakdown.ct` counts reposts by crypto-Twitter accounts.

parameters.description: Up to 5 keywords to search for, separated by commas (optional if accountName provided)
parameters.name: keywords

parameters.description: Account username to filter by (optional if keywords provided)
parameters.name: accountName

parameters.description: Cursor for pagination (millisecond timestamp from a previous response's `metadata.cursor`)
parameters.name: cursor

get.description:
Generate event summaries based on keyword mentions within a time period.
This endpoint costs 5 credits total
get.summary: Event summary from keyword mentions (5 credits total)

parameters.description: Keywords to search for, separated by commas
parameters.name: keywords

This endpoint costs 5 credits total

parameters.name: maxNarratives

parameters.name: maxTweetsPerNarrative

parameters.description: CoinGecko Coin IDs to filter by, comma-separated
parameters.name: coinIds

get.description:
Get trending contract addresses mentioned on Twitter/X. Returns contract addresses with mention counts.

Note: You must provide EITHER timeWindow OR both from and to parameters.
If both timeWindow and from/to are provided, from/to takes priority.

Get trending contract addresses mentioned on Telegram. Returns contract addresses with mention counts.

Note: You must provide EITHER timeWindow OR both from and to parameters.
If both timeWindow and from/to are provided, from/to takes priority.

post.description:
Send a message to the Elfa AI and receive a complete response.
Suited for agents and integrations that do not support server-sent events (SSE).

**Session management:** omit `sessionId` to start a new conversation.
The response includes a `sessionId` — pass it in subsequent requests to continue the same conversation.

**Analysis types and required fields:**
- `chat` (default) — general conversation. Requires `message`.
- `macro` — macro market overview. `message` is optional.
- `summary` — quick market summary. `message` is optional.
- `tokenIntro` — token introduction. Requires `assetMetadata` with `symbol`, or `chain` + `contractAddress`.
- `tokenAnalysis` — token trade setup analysis. Same `assetMetadata` requirements as `tokenIntro`.
- `accountAnalysis` — Twitter/X account analysis. Requires `assetMetadata.username`.

**`assetMetadata` fields (when applicable):**
- `symbol` — token ticker symbol (e.g. `"BTC"`, `"$ETH"`)
- `chain` — blockchain name (e.g. `"ethereum"`, `"solana"`)
- `contractAddress` — token contract address
- `username` — Twitter/X username for account analysis

value.message: Summarize BTC sentiment in the last 24h.

403.description: Auto is not enabled for this API key

post.description:
Ask the AI to help you build an EQL query.

The response message contains the AI's reply in Markdown. When it generates EQL,
it will be in a JSON code block that you can extract, validate, and submit.

Use `sessionId` in follow-up requests to maintain conversation context.

### Cost

API mode: `1 + dynamic` credits
- base request charge: `1` credit
- dynamic usage charge: `ceil(request_cost * 750)` credits

In x402 mode, equivalent reference pricing remains:
- `fast`: `5 credits` (`$0.045`)
- `expert`: `18 credits` (`$0.162`)

### Auth

Required header:
```http
x-elfa-api-key: <api_key>
```

This route does not require HMAC. Chat produces drafts only; any
subsequent activation goes through `POST /queries/drafts/{draftId}/convert`,
which gates trade-flavoured drafts behind HMAC.

### Request Example

```json
{
  "message": "Alert me when BTC breaks 100k",
  "speed": "expert",
  "sessionId": "optional-session-id"
}
```

### Response Example (200)

```json
{
  "sessionId": "session-uuid",
  "response": "I can help with that...",
  "title": "BTC Breakout Alert",
  "reasoning": null,
  "planIds": []
}
```

> Best practice: persist `sessionId` and reuse it for follow-up prompts so
> the model keeps context across turns.

value.message: Build an Auto query for BTC breakout above 100k.

403.description: Auto is not enabled for this API key

get.description:
Check whether a symbol is tradable as a Hyperliquid perpetual.

Use this before submitting an EQL query whose action is `market_order` or
`limit_order` (or an `llm` callback to either) — those actions only execute
on perps, and the scheduler rejects symbols not present in Hyperliquid's
perp universe. Spot-only tokens (e.g. `HFUN`) return `"false"` here even
though they may still be queryable in price/sentiment conditions.

### Cost

Free.

### Auth

Required header:
```http
x-elfa-api-key: <api_key>
```

HMAC signing is not required for this check.

### Response Example (200)

```json
{
  "supported": "true"
}
```

403.description: Auto is not enabled for this API key

value.title: BTC breakout alert
value.description: Notify when BTC is above 100k so I can review the breakout setup.

403.description: Auto is not enabled for this API key

post.description:
Create and activate a conditional query.

### Required Sequence (Enforced)

Call `POST /v2/auto/queries/validate` immediately before create.

For trade actions (`market_order`, `limit_order`), preflight
`GET /v2/auto/exchanges` before create.

Lifecycle after create:
1. `POST /v2/auto/queries/{queryId}/cancel` while status is `active`.
2. `DELETE /v2/auto/queries/{queryId}` only when status is terminal
   (`triggered`, `expired`, `cancelled`, `failed`).

**Allowed action types (API key mode):**
`webhook`, `notify`, `telegram_bot`, `llm`, `market_order`, `limit_order`.

Trade actions (`market_order`, `limit_order`) require an active exchange
connection for the linked user (for example Hyperliquid via
`POST /v2/auto/exchanges`). Without a connected exchange, query creation
can succeed but order execution fails when the trigger fires.

### Cost

Simulation-driven estimate (reference values):
- baseline: `5 credits` (`$0.045`)
- fast call: `+5 credits` (`+$0.045`)
- expert call: `+18 credits` (`+$0.162`)

### Auth

Required header (always):
```http
x-elfa-api-key: <api_key>
```

**HMAC is conditional** — required when the request body's action is
trade-flavoured (`market_order`, `limit_order`, or `llm` whose
`params.callback.action.type` is `market_order` or `limit_order`). HMAC is
NOT required for notification-only actions (`notify`, `telegram_bot`,
`webhook`, or `llm` with a notification callback). Unknown action types
default to requiring HMAC.

When HMAC is required, also send:
```http
x-elfa-timestamp: <unix_seconds>
x-elfa-signature: <hex_hmac_sha256>
```

Signing payload: `timestamp + method + path + body`. For this route, sign
with `path = "/queries"`. Always-signing clients are supported — HMAC sent
on a notification-only request is accepted.

### Request Example

```json
{
  "query": {
    "conditions": {
      "AND": [
        {
          "source": "price",
          "method": "current",
          "args": { "symbol": "BTC" },
          "operator": ">",
          "value": 100000
        }
      ]
    },
    "actions": [
      {
        "stepId": "step_1",
        "type": "notify",
        "params": { "message": "BTC crossed target" }
      }
    ],
    "expiresIn": "24h"
  },
  "title": "BTC breakout alert",
  "description": "Notify when BTC is above 100k"
}
```

### Response Example (201)

```json
{
  "queryId": "q_123",
  "status": "active",
  "cost": {
    "credits": 116,
    "price": "$1.045"
  }
}
```

> Best practice: call validate first, then create. After creation, wire
> notifications (`webhook`, `telegram_bot`, or SSE) to a background runner.

value.title: BTC breakout alert
value.description: Notify when BTC is above 100k so I can review the breakout setup.

get.description: List Auto queries for the authenticated API-key user.

Check query status and execution results.

### Cost

Free.

### Auth

Required header:
```http
x-elfa-api-key: <api_key>
```

### Request Example

Path parameter:
- `queryId` (string)

### Response Example (200)

```json
{
  "queryId": "q_123",
  "status": "active",
  "latestEvaluation": {
    "evaluatedAt": "2026-04-01T12:00:00.000Z",
    "wouldTriggerNow": false
  },
  "executions": [
    {
      "id": "exec_123",
      "queryId": "q_123",
      "type": "llm",
      "status": "failed",
      "error": {
        "code": "LLM_ACTION_UPSTREAM_ERROR",
        "message": "Failed to execute llm action"
      },
      "createdAt": "2026-04-01T12:00:01.000Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

> Best practice: use polling for debugging or backfills. For production
> delivery, prefer webhook/SSE notifications.

parameters.description: Query or draft id (drafts resolve via the same path as running queries)
parameters.name: queryId

409.description: Query not in a terminal state

delete.description:
Delete a terminal query.

Only queries in a terminal status (`triggered`, `expired`, `cancelled`,
`failed`) can be deleted. Active queries must be cancelled first.
Do not use this endpoint to cancel running queries; use
`POST /v2/auto/queries/{queryId}/cancel` for active queries.

### Cost

Free.

### Auth

Required header (always):
```http
x-elfa-api-key: <api_key>
```

**HMAC is conditional** — required when the *stored* query has a
trade-flavoured action (`market_order`, `limit_order`, or `llm` callback to
those). NOT required when the stored query is notification-only. If the
stored query cannot be looked up, HMAC is enforced (fail-safe).

When HMAC is required, also send:
```http
x-elfa-timestamp: <unix_seconds>
x-elfa-signature: <hex_hmac_sha256>
```

Signing payload: `timestamp + method + path + body`. For this route, sign
with `path = "/queries/{queryId}"` and `body = ""`.

### Request Example

Path parameter:
- `queryId` (string)

### Response Example (200)

```json
{
  "id": "q_123",
  "deletedAt": "2026-04-01T12:00:00.000Z"
}
```

get.description:
Stream query notifications via Server-Sent Events (SSE).

### Cost

Free.

### Auth

Required header:
```http
x-elfa-api-key: <api_key>
```

### Request Example

Path parameter:
- `queryId` (string)

### Response Example (200)

- `Content-Type: text/event-stream`
- stream remains open and emits incremental SSE events

> Best practice: deduplicate SSE events by event id and process with a
> queue-backed worker for reliable agent execution.

409.description: Query not in a cancellable state

post.description:
Cancel an active query.

Only queries in `active` status can be cancelled. Queries
already in a terminal state return 409 Conflict.
This endpoint does not delete query records; use
`DELETE /v2/auto/queries/{queryId}` after a terminal status when you need
soft-delete behavior.

### Cost

Free.

### Auth

Required header (always):
```http
x-elfa-api-key: <api_key>
```

**HMAC is conditional** — required when the *stored* query has a
trade-flavoured action (`market_order`, `limit_order`, or `llm` callback to
those). NOT required when the stored query is notification-only. If the
stored query cannot be looked up, HMAC is enforced (fail-safe).

When HMAC is required, also send:
```http
x-elfa-timestamp: <unix_seconds>
x-elfa-signature: <hex_hmac_sha256>
```

Signing payload: `timestamp + method + path + body`. For this route, sign
with `path = "/queries/{queryId}/cancel"` and `body = ""`.

### Request Example

Path parameter:
- `queryId` (string)

### Response Example (200)

```json
{
  "id": "q_123",
  "status": "cancelled",
  "cancelledAt": "2026-04-01T12:00:00.000Z"
}
```

> Best practice: keep cancellation idempotent in your client workflow;
> treat repeated cancel attempts as safe no-op behavior.

get.description:
List LLM output sessions for a query.
Only relevant for queries with `actions[0].type: "llm"`.

### Cost

Free.

### Auth

Required header:
```http
x-elfa-api-key: <api_key>
```

### Request Example

Path parameter:
- `queryId` (string)

### Response Example (200)

```json
{
  "queryId": "q_123",
  "sessions": [
    {
      "sessionId": "s_1",
      "status": "completed",
      "executedAt": "2026-04-01T12:00:00.000Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

> Best practice: store `sessionId` values from executions so you can fetch
> full analysis records only when needed.

Get full LLM analysis output for a specific session.

### Cost

Free.

### Auth

Required header:
```http
x-elfa-api-key: <api_key>
```

### Request Example

Path parameters:
- `queryId` (string)
- `sessionId` (string)

### Response Example (200)

```json
{
  "sessionId": "s_1",
  "queryId": "q_123",
  "title": "Token analysis",
  "analysisType": "tokenAnalysis",
  "createdAt": "2026-04-01T12:00:00.000Z",
  "messages": [
    {
      "query": "Analyze BTC",
      "response": "...",
      "status": "completed",
      "analysisType": "tokenAnalysis",
      "timestamp": "2026-04-01T12:00:00.000Z",
      "trades": []
    }
  ]
}
```

> Best practice: treat top-level and message-level fields as nullable and
> handle partial records defensively in your parser.

post.description:
Create or update a query draft.

### Auth

Required header (always):
```http
x-elfa-api-key: <api_key>
```

**HMAC is conditional** — required when the request body's action is
trade-flavoured (`market_order`, `limit_order`, or `llm` callback to
those). NOT required for notification-only actions. The gate exists to
prevent a leaked API key from race-replacing a notification-only draft
with a trade draft before it is converted.

When HMAC is required, also send:
```http
x-elfa-timestamp: <unix_seconds>
x-elfa-signature: <hex_hmac_sha256>
```

Signing payload: `timestamp + method + path + body`. For this route, sign
with `path = "/queries/drafts"`.

trade-flavoured action (`market_order`, `limit_order`, or `llm` callback
to those). NOT required when the draft is notification-only. If the draft
cannot be looked up, HMAC is enforced (fail-safe).

When HMAC is required, also send:

```http
x-elfa-signature: <hex_hmac_sha256>
```

Signing payload: `timestamp + method + path + body`. For this route, sign
with `path = "/queries/drafts/{draftId}/convert"` and `body = ""`.
